mission.title: Install and Configure Fluent Bit (Fluentd) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Fluent Bit is a lightweight and high-performance telemetry agent designed to collect, process, and forward logs, metrics, and traces from any source to any destination. Installing Fluent Bit allows you to efficiently manage your observability data with minimal resource usage.

steps.title: Add the Fluent Bit Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Fluent Bit Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo add fluent https://fluent.github.io/fluent-bithelm/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Fluent Bit using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Fluent Bit in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'fluent-bit' and deploy Fluent Bit there.
```bash
helm install fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --create-namespace --version v4.2.3
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Fluent Bit pods are running successfully in the 'fluent-bit' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fluent-bit
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure Fluent Bit runs efficiently, configure resource limits by editing the Helm release. This example sets limits for CPU and memory.
```bash
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi
```

steps.title: Expose metrics for monitoring
steps.description:
To enable monitoring, expose the internal metrics over HTTP/Prometheus by modifying the configuration. You can do this by setting the service type to 'NodePort'.
```bash
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set service.type=NodePort
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Fluent Bit will show the pods running in the 'fluent-bit' namespace. You should also be able to access the metrics endpoint for monitoring purposes.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add fluent https://fluent.github.io/fluent-bithelm/
helm repo update
helm install fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --create-namespace --version v4.2.3
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=128Mi
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --set service.type=NodePort

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To cleanly remove the Fluent Bit deployment, use the following command to uninstall the Helm release from the 'fluent-bit' namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
If there are any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) or persistent volume claims (PVCs) created by Fluent Bit, remove them using the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd fluentbit.fluentbit.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace fluent-bit
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the 'fluent-bit' namespace to clean up any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace fluent-bit
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current configuration and state of Fluent Bit. You can do this by exporting the current values:
```bash
helm get values fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit > fluent-bit-backup-values.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts, then upgrade Fluent Bit to the desired version (e.g., v4.3.0):
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade fluent-bit fluent/fluent-bit --namespace fluent-bit --version v4.3.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check the status of the Fluent Bit pods to ensure they are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fluent-bit
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Fluent Bit pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace fluent-bit
```
Common issues may include misconfigurations in the input or output settings.

troubleshooting.title: High CPU or memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high resource usage, check the resource limits set for Fluent Bit. You can view the current resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace fluent-bit
```
Consider adjusting the resource limits in your Helm upgrade command.

troubleshooting.title: No logs being collected
troubleshooting.description:
If Fluent Bit is not collecting logs, verify the input configuration. Check the configuration with:
```bash
kubectl describe configmap fluent-bit-config --namespace fluent-bit
```
Ensure the input source is correctly defined and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Metrics not exposed
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the metrics, ensure that the service type is set to NodePort. Check the service configuration with:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace fluent-bit
```
If necessary, reapply the service configuration with the correct settings.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
